name: Bangkok (Thailand)
description: A green field charged at the centre with the seal of Bangkok, with the province name in Thai script.
history: Bangkok (Krung Thep Maha Nakhon) is a Special Administrative Area of Thailand (one of two, with Pattaya). Its flag bears the seal of the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration on a green field; the central image depicts Indra mounted on Erawan, the three-headed elephant of Hindu-Buddhist mythology, brandishing his vajra. The seal has been used by the city's administration since the 1970s.

features.role: Seal of the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration
features.symbolism: Indra (the Hindu sky-god, in Thai tradition the city's protector) mounted on Erawan, the three-headed elephant, brandishing the vajra (thunderbolt) — symbolising the divine guardianship of Krung Thep, the City of Angels.
